data.title: Whales Accumulate Bitcoin as New Wallets Withdraw 851 BTC
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent monitoring by Lookonchain reveals that whales are actively accumulating Bitcoin. On September 19, two newly created wallets withdrew a total of 851 BTC from a trading platform, amounting to a value of $99 million. This activity highlights a significant movement in the cryptocurrency market as large investors continue to increase their Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: Significant Bitcoin Withdrawal Observed Amid Market Movements
data.description: According to TechFlow, on September 19, two newly created wallets withdrew 851 BTC, valued at approximately $99 million, from a centralized exchange (CEX), as monitored by the on-chain analysis platform Lookonchain. This substantial movement of Bitcoin highlights ongoing shifts within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: WLFI passed the 100% fee repurchase and destruction proposal
data.description: World Liberty Financial (WLFI), a crypto project backed by the Trump family, has passed a proposal to use all fees generated from the protocol's own liquidity to repurchase and permanently destroy WLFI tokens. Voting results showed that 99.84% of votes supported the proposal.

data.title: AIA hit 0.547 USDT, up over 42% in 24 hours
data.description: Market data shows that the crypto market is generally experiencing a correction today. However, DeAgentAI (AIA), an AI project from the Sui ecosystem that launched on Binance yesterday, bucked the trend and surged. The AIA token surged by over 42% over the past 24 hours, rising from around 0.35 USDT to a high of 0.547 USDT.

data.title: Fed's Kashkari: Two more rate cuts this year would be appropriate
data.description: Fed's Kashkari: Two more rate cuts this year would be appropriate. If the labor market weakens more than expected, we can always cut rates more quickly. We are willing to raise the policy rate if economic conditions warrant. (Jinshi)

data.title: FG Nexus Expands Ethereum Holdings to 49,715 ETH
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, FG Nexus, a publicly traded company on the U.S. stock market, has announced an increase in its Ethereum holdings by 1,087 ETH. As of September 18, the company's total Ethereum holdings have reached 49,715 ETH, valued at approximately $229 million based on the current price of $4,598 per ETH.

FG Nexus CEO Maja emphasized that Ethereum is the preferred programmable asset for institutional reserves. The company is executing a strategy to become a leading corporate holder of Ethereum. FG Nexus plans to enhance its Ethereum returns through staking and re-staking methods and will further explore Ethereum-driven financial solutions.

data.title: Bank of Italy Official Warns of Stablecoin Risks to EU Financial Stability
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a senior official from the Bank of Italy has raised concerns about the potential risks posed by stablecoins issued by multiple entities across different countries to the European Union's financial system. Chiara Scotti, vice director of the Bank of Italy, emphasized that unless these stablecoins are restricted to jurisdictions with equivalent regulatory standards, they could present significant legal, operational, liquidity, and financial stability risks. Scotti's remarks were made during the Economics of Payments Conference held in Rome on September 18.

Scotti elaborated on the potential dangers of multi-issuance stablecoins, which are digital tokens issued in several countries under a single brand. While these stablecoins could enhance global liquidity and scalability, they also bring considerable risks, especially if at least one issuer operates outside the EU. To mitigate these risks, Scotti recommended that such stablecoins should be confined to jurisdictions with similar regulatory frameworks and ensure redemption at par. Additionally, she stressed the importance of enforcing cross-jurisdictional crisis protocols to maintain stability.

Despite the risks, Scotti acknowledged the potential benefits of stablecoins, describing them as promising tools for reducing transaction costs, improving efficiency, and providing 24/7 availability. However, she argued that only stablecoins pegged to a single fiat currency are suitable as payment instruments due to their ability to offer a high level of customer protection through the right to redemption at their nominal value. This highlights the need for strong cross-border cooperation among supervisory authorities to consistently monitor and verify the adequacy of reserves.

Italian regulators have expressed concerns over the growing prominence of stablecoins. The country's financial markets regulator, Commissione Nazionale per le Società e la Borsa, has joined forces with regulators in France and Austria to advocate for transferring regulatory supervision of crypto firms to the Paris-based European Securities and Markets Authority. In May, Fabio Panetta, a former European Central Bank official and Governor of the Bank of Italy, suggested that a euro-based central bank digital currency could be a more effective tool for addressing the risks associated with increasing cryptocurrency adoption, rather than regulating cryptocurrencies. This followed a report by the Bank of Italy in April, which identified stablecoins and non-financial firms' crypto exposure as key concerns. The report warned of potential systemic risks if dollar-pegged tokens were to become widespread, noting that disruptions in stablecoins or the underlying US government bonds could have repercussions for other parts of the global financial system. Additionally, Italy's Minister of Economy and Finance, Giancarlo Giorgetti, cautioned that US stablecoin policies might threaten the euro's dominance.

data.title: Fed's Kashkari Discusses Potential Rate Adjustments Amid Economic Conditions
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Federal Reserve official Neel Kashkari stated that if the labor market weakens more than anticipated, the Fed could expedite interest rate cuts. He noted that it is unlikely for inflation to significantly exceed 3% due to tariffs. Kashkari expressed willingness to raise policy rates if economic conditions necessitate such action. He suggested that the neutral interest rate might have increased to 3.1% and remarked that the Fed's policy is not as restrictive as previously thought. While inflation may persist, a substantial rise is considered improbable.

data.title: WLFI Protocol Implements Permanent Token Burn Strategy
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, World Liberty Financial's proposal to use all fees generated from WLFI protocol-owned liquidity (POL) for public market buybacks and permanent token destruction has been approved, with 99.84% of voters in favor.

The term 'protocol-owned liquidity' refers exclusively to fees generated from liquidity controlled by WLFI, excluding fees from community and third-party liquidity providers. Essentially, WLFI collects fees from its liquidity positions on Ethereum, BSC, and Solana chains, using these fees to repurchase WLFI tokens in the market. The repurchased tokens are then sent to a burn address, resulting in a permanent reduction in supply.

The official statement indicates that if the proposal is implemented, WLFI will use this as the foundation for a continuous buyback and burn strategy. As the ecosystem develops, there are plans to explore incorporating other protocol revenue sources into this initiative, gradually expanding the scale of WLFI buybacks and token destruction.

data.title: Pump.fun live streaming concept coins plummeted, with the leading market value only $5 million.
data.description: According to OKX Wallet data, the value of Pump.fun live-streaming concept tokens has plummeted, leaving only six with a market capitalization exceeding $1 million. Bagwork, previously the leading token, boasted a market capitalization exceeding $50 million, but is now reported at $5 million, a 90% drop. The live-streaming concept token with the highest market capitalization is KIND, currently reported at $6 million.
